startassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the start offset, to determine the start measure for this linear segment.
startassetfeatureid.title: Start Asset Feature ID

lastcompdate.description: Latest completion date of any work order that is generated from this PM. This field is automatically updated when a PM work order is completed or closed.
lastcompdate.title: Last Completion Date

parentchgsstatus.description: Specifies whether the child work order should change its status when the parents status changes. If the check box is selected, the status of the child work orders will change. If the check box is cleared, the status of the child work orders will not change.
parentchgsstatus.title: Child Work Orders and Tasks Will Inherit Status Changes

ownergroup.description: The person group who will be responsible for the work order generated from this PM.
ownergroup.title: Owner Group

startmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where the work for this PM will take place.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering a start reference point and start offset.
startmeasure.title: Start Measure

endoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the end reference point of the asset on this PM.
endoffsetunitid.title: Unit of End Offset

usefrequency.description: Tells the Generate WO function that the frequency for a lower level PM should be checked when determining if all of the PMs in the hierarchy should be generated.
usefrequency.title: Use this PM to Trigger PM Hierarchy

endzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
endzoffset.title: End Z Offset

stconoffset.description: The amount of time (in hours) to be subtracted from the Target Start date in order to calculate the Start No Earlier Than date of the work record.
stconoffset.title: Start Constraint Offset

pmassetwogen.description: Flag that indicates to generate work orders automatically from a PM when meter frequency is reached.
pmassetwogen.title: Generate Work Order When Meter Frequency is Reached

fnconoffset.description: The amount of time (in hours) to be added to the Target Finish date in order to calculate the Finish No Later Than date of the work record.
fnconoffset.title: Finish Constraint Offset

ltdpmcounter.description: Number of work orders generated from the PM since the First Start Date. The counter is set to zero when you insert a new PM record, and increases each time you generate a top-level work order from the PM. If you are using a job plan sequence, the job plan is selected after the counter increments. Life to Date Counter cannot be reset by Set PM Counter.
ltdpmcounter.title: Life To Date Counter

schedearly.description: Check box determines how conflicts between the PM frequency and the day of the week are resolved.  If the check box is selected, then the PM work order is conservatively assigned a Target Start on a selected day of the week that is earlier or the same as the normal frequency-based schedule date.  If the check box is cleared, the PM work order will only be assigned a Target Start on a selected day of the week that is the same as or later than the normal frequency-based schedule date.
schedearly.title: Schedule Early on Frequency Conflict

priority.description: Priority of work orders generated from this PM.
priority.title: Priority

pmfpcostcaldate.description: The date when the PM forecast cost was last calculated.
pmfpcostcaldate.title: Last Calculated Date

sunday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
sunday.title: Sunday

masterpm.description: Identifies the master PM record associated with this PM.
masterpm.title: Master PM

route.description: Identifies the route associated with the PM. A route is a list of stops that represent asset or work locations. When you generate work orders from a PM with an associated route, you create a parent work order for the asset shown in the Asset or Location field or for the GL account, and a child work order for each asset listed on the route.
route.title: Route

pmnum.description: Identifies the PM. This value must be unique for all PM records.
pmnum.title: PM

leadtime.description: The number of days in advance of the Next Due Date that Maximo generates work orders from this PM. The target start date for the work order will still be the Next Due Date. You must Select the Lead Time Active checkbox before you can enter or edit data in this field.
leadtime.title: Lead Time (Days)

reqasstdwntime.description: Indicates if the asset requires downtime while work is performed.
reqasstdwntime.title: Require Asset Downtime

amcrew.description: Identifies the crew that performed the work.
amcrew.title: Crew

targstarttime.description: Target time for starting the PM Work Order.
targstarttime.title: Target Start Time

apptrequired.description: Indicates if an appointment is required for the job plan. Tasks that are created for this job plan inherit this value, but can be modified.
apptrequired.title: Appointment Required

pluscchangedate.description: Displays the date the status was changed. This field is editable only on the Change Status dialog box.
pluscchangedate.title: Changed Date

pluscrevnum.description: Displays the revision level of the current job plan.
pluscrevnum.title: Revision

aos.description: Indicates that the work cannot be performed while the asset is operating.
aos.title: Requires Asset Downtime

calcapplyto.description: Calculation type : used to specify if the calculation update the duration or quantity value
calcapplyto.title: Type

pluscrevcom.description: Enter the reason for the change. This field is editable only when the revision is first created.
pluscrevcom.title: Description

ownergroup.description: The person group who will be responsible for the work order created with this job plan
ownergroup.title: Owner Group

lms.description: Indicates that the work must be performed during a dedicated maintenance window.
lms.title: Requires Location Maintenance Window

stconoffset.description: The amount of time (in hours) to be subtracted from the Target Start date in order to calculate the Start No Earlier Than date of the work record.
stconoffset.title: Start Constraint Offset

pluscstatusdate.description: Displays the date the status was changed. This field is editable only on the Change Status dialog box.
pluscstatusdate.title: Last status change date

dynamic.description: Flag used to hide or display dynamic fields
dynamic.title: Is dynamic

flowaction.description: The action to be performed (if any) when the workorder is started via process flow control
flowaction.title: Flow Action

suspendflow.description: Flag used to suspend and resume flow control on a parent record.
suspendflow.title: Suspend Flow Control

fnconoffset.description: The amount of time (in hours) to be added to the Target Finish date in order to calculate the Finish No Later Than date of the work record.
fnconoffset.title: Finish Constraint Offset

modelid.description: Import ID Typically from a BIM model or COBie data set.
modelid.title: Import ID

repairlocflag.description: Indicates whether the repair location is required.
repairlocflag.title: Repair Facility Required

laborcode.description: Defines the Lead person who will be copied to the work order created with this job plan
laborcode.title: Lead

pluscchangeby.description: Displays the user name of the person logged in when the job plan was created or changed.
pluscchangeby.title: Changed By

intshift.description: Work will be interrupted for only this shift in Graphical Scheduling application if value supplied. (Organization required)
intshift.title: Interruptible shift

flowactionassist.description: Suppresses the automatic firing of the action in flow control
flowactionassist.title: Flow Action Assist

reqasstdwntime.description: Indicates if the asset requires downtime while work is performed.
reqasstdwntime.title: Require Asset Downtime

amcrew.description: Defines the Crew who will be copied to the work order created with this job plan.
amcrew.title: Crew

inctasksinsched.description: Indicates that tasks of records created from this job plan will be included in the schedule view.
inctasksinsched.title: Include Tasks in Schedule

flowcontrolled.description: Flag used to indicate a records participation in flow control.
flowcontrolled.title: Flow Controlled

ams.description: Indicates that the work must be performed during a dedicated maintenance window.
ams.title: Requires Asset Maintenance Window

jpduration.description: Duration Of Job Plan In Hours
jpduration.title: Duration

description.description: One Line Short Description Of Job Plan
description.title: Description

assignedownergroup.description: Identifies the owner group who will be responsible for the work order created with this job plan.  Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner group. You can enter a value either in this field or in the Owner field.
assignedownergroup.title: Assigned Owner Group

los.description: Indicates that the work must be performed when the location is not in use, and available for maintenance.
los.title: Requires Location Downtime

calcmethod.description: Calculation method; It could be STATIC, PROPORTIONAL, LEVEL or ATTRIBUTE
calcmethod.title: Calculation

jp10.description: Program this Job Plan is for

templatetype.description: Template Type of the Job Plan
templatetype.title: Template Type

crewworkgroup.description: Resource pool that will perform the work order.

jp14.description: If SMP, is it Minor Maintenance?

crewid.description: ID for a given crew within a craft.

calendar.description: Calendar to determine shift work is to be done on.
calendar.title: Calendar

owner.description: The person who will be responsible for the work order created with this job plan
owner.title: Owner

applynondynamiccalc.description: If the work order asset is a linear one, and that check box is checked, the TWU will not be calculated but entered by user
applynondynamiccalc.title: Ignore linear measures

interruptible.description: Is this WO allowed to be stopped and restarted during resource scheduling
interruptible.title: Interruptible

downtime.description: Does the Asset have to be down for this WO?
downtime.title: Downtime

hasnestedjp.description: Indicates if this Job Plan has nested Job Plans.
hasnestedjp.title: Nested Job Plans

pmcombpelenabled.description: Is this object currently part of a BPEL flow
pmcombpelenabled.title: BPEL Enabled

inspformnum.description: The number of the inspection form.
inspformnum.title: Inspection Form

copywheninactive.description: Should this PM be copied to the new asset that is created on receipt of a cross-site or cross-organization asset transfer even if status is INACTIVE? The system uses this flag to determine which PMs became INACTIVE when the asset was SHIPPED so that the PMs are copied to the asset created in the new site or organization when the shipment is received. This flag pertains only to PMs created from Master PMs because these are the only PMs that are moved cross-site or cross-organization with an asset when it is transferred.
copywheninactive.title: Copy When Inactive on Receipt of Transferred Asset

saturday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
saturday.title: Saturday

storelocsite.description: Specifies the Storeroom's Site of an item
storelocsite.title: Storeroom Site

assignedownergroup.description: Identifies the owner group who will be responsible for the work order created with this PM. Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner group. You can enter a value either in this field or the Owner field.
assignedownergroup.title: Assigned Owner Group

assetnum.description: Asset on which the preventive maintenance is done.

alertlead.description: Acceptable period (in days), prior to the PM due date, during which the PM may be performed.
alertlead.title: Alert Lead (Days)

crewworkgroup.description: Identifies the resource pool to which the labor belongs.
crewworkgroup.title: Crew Work Group

persongroup.description: Identifies the Person Group for the supervisor.
persongroup.title: Work Group

startzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
startzoffset.title: Start Z Offset

crewid.description: Work crew assigned to work orders generated from this PM. Maximo copies this field from either a single job plan on the PM, or from the first job plan in a job plan sequence. If you change the job plan associated with the PM, you must update this field manually.
crewid.title: Crew

wednesday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
wednesday.title: Wednesday

startzoffsetref.description: Reference point from which the start Z-Offset (distance above or below the linear asset) is measured.

erlstwogendate.description: Earliest next due date for a work order generated from this PM.

monday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
monday.title: Monday

calendar.description: Calendar to determine shift work is to be done on.
calendar.title: Calendar

description_longdescription.description: Long Description for One Line Short Description Of PM
description_longdescription.title: Details

extdate.description: If Extended Date supplied it overrides Next Due Date.
extdate.title: Extended Date

basemeasureunitid.description: Usually, this is the same as the unit of measure  for the asset. If they differ, Maximo will display measures in the unit of measure but will store measures in both the unit of measure and base unit of measure.  A conversion between the unit of measure and base unit of measure, entered via the Units of Measure and Conversion Action in Assets, must exist if the base unit of measure differs from the unit of measure.
basemeasureunitid.title: Unit of Base Measure

endmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where this PM segment ends.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering an end reference point and end offset.
endmeasure.title: End Measure

startyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
startyoffset.title: Start Y Offset

thursday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
thursday.title: Thursday

interruptible.description: Specifies if the work order created from this PM is allowed to be stopped and restarted during resource scheduling. If the checkbox is selected, the work order can be stopped and restarted. If the checkbox is cleared, the work order cannot be interrupted for resource scheduling.
interruptible.title: Interruptible

overridemasterupd.description: Specifies whether changes to the Master PM will update this PM. If the checkbox is selected, changes to the Master PM will not affect this PM. If the checkbox is cleared, changes to the master PM will roll down to this PM whenever the Update Associated PMs action is performed.
overridemasterupd.title: Override Updates from Master PM

lead.description: The lead person for the work order generated from this PM.

inclforecast.description: Indicates if this PM  should be included in the forecast

downtime.description: Does the Asset have to be down to perform work order?
downtime.title: Downtime

endyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
endyoffset.title: End Y Offset

usetargetdate.description: Select this check box if you want to use the target start date of the last PM work order to calculate the due date of the next PM work order. Clear this check box if you want to use the completion date of the last PM work order to calculate the next due date.
usetargetdate.title: Use Last Work Order's Start Date to Calculate Next Due Date

adjnextdue.description: Adjust the next due date after generation?
adjnextdue.title: Adjust Next Due Date

pluscsumeu.description: This field is summed with the %Span, %URV, and %Reading EU fields to obtain the total accuracy of the asset.

pluscsumspan.description: This field is summed with the %Reading, %URV, and Accuracy EU fields to obtain the total accuracy of the asset.
pluscsumspan.title: %SPAN

startmeasure.description: A value that identifies the start of the linear asset.  The absolute value of the end measure minus the start measure will determine the length of the linear asset.  The start measure and the end measure also determine the boundary measures for any features or relationships applied to the asset.
startmeasure.title: Start Measure

ame_avgfive.description: The average annual cost for this asset. Calculated using costs over the last 5 years, based on your defined fiscal year.

pluscisinhousecal.description: When selected indicates that this tool is calibrated in house. The default is cleared.

ftwo.description: Indicates if the Condition record is part of the Fast Track work flow

pluscoprgeto.description: Enter the maximum numeric value of the operating range of the tool. This field is used in conjuction with the Operating Range From field.

depreciationpending.description: Indicates that generation of the depreciation schedule is pending final receipt or invoice of the PO that this rotating asset was purchased on.

groupname.description: All meters belonging to this asset's metergroup are automatically associated with this Asset in the AssetMeter object. Additions to the meter group may also be added to this Asset's AssetMeters.

frequency.title: Frequency

pluscduedate.description: Determines which PM is the one that establishes the Assets next calibration due date
pluscduedate.title: Due Date

endbasemeasure.description: An absolute measure that is calculated by converting from the end measure using a defined conversion method.  Conversion methods are defined in the Assets application using the Add/Modify Conversions action.
endbasemeasure.title: End Base Measure

repairlocflag.description: Indicates whether the repair location is required.
repairlocflag.title: Repair Facility Required

reforecast.description: Indicates if subsequent dates for this PM should be adjusted as a result of the New Date entered. If you select this checkbox, the forecast dates following the adjusted date will be reforecasted automatically. If you clear this checkbox, subsequent PM dates in the forecast will remain unchanged. If a forecast exists for this PM, this field is read-only.
reforecast.title: Reforecast Subsequent Dates

jpseqinuse.description: Specifies whether the PM uses job plan sequences. If the check box is selected, the PM generates different work orders each time based on a job plan sequence. If the checkbox is cleared, the PM generates identical work orders every time.
jpseqinuse.title: Use Job Plan Sequences

startoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the start reference point of the asset on this PM.
startoffsetunitid.title: Unit of Start Offset

startoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the start reference point to determine where the PM work location begins.
startoffset.title: Start Offset

endassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the end offset, to determine the end measure for this linear segment.
endassetfeatureid.title: End Asset Feature ID

pmcounter.description: Number of work orders generated from the PM since the First Start Date. The counter is set to zero when you insert a new PM record, and increases each time you generate a top-level work order from the PM. If you are using a job plan sequence, the job plan is selected after the counter increments.
pmcounter.title: Counter

supervisor.description: Person or craft responsible for implementing the work order. Maximo copies this field from either a single job plan on the PM, or from the first job plan in a job plan sequence. If you change the job plan associated with the PM, you must update this field manually.
supervisor.title: Supervisor

lockforecast.description: Indicates if New Dates can be modified or added to the forecast. If the check box is selected, no New Dates can be added or modified. To modify or add New Dates, select Unlock Forecast Dates from the Select Action menu.
lockforecast.title: Forecast Dates Locked

description.description: Describes the PM. To enter or view additional information, click the Long Description button.
description.title: Description

endoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the end reference point to determine where the PM work location ends.
endoffset.title: End Offset

nextdate.description: If you selected the Use Last Work Order's Start Information to Calculate Next Due Date check box, it is the date that is calculated by adding the frequency value to the target start date of the last work order. If you did not select that check box, it is calculated by adding the frequency value to the completion date of the last work order.
nextdate.title: Estimated Next Due Date

endyoffsetref.description: The point from which the Y Offset is measured.  The user can create a domain for this purpose.  The value entered will not be used in calculations -- it is simply used to locate the feature in the field.
endyoffsetref.title: End Y Offset Referent

friday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
friday.title: Friday

wosequence.description: WO Sequence number. Copied to WorkOrder.WOSequence.
wosequence.title: Sequence

owner.description: The person who will be responsible for the work order generated from this PM.
owner.title: Owner

startyoffsetref.description: The point from which the Y Offset is measured.  The user can create a domain for this purpose.  The value entered will not be used in calculations -- it is simply used to locate the feature in the field.
startyoffsetref.title: Start Y Offset Referent

storeloc.description: The storeroom for materials on a PM with an associated job plan. If you leave this field blank, Maximo will populate this field with your default storeroom.
storeloc.title: Storeroom

worktype.description: Classification or type of work order generated from this PM.
worktype.title: Work Type

grandtotalcost.description: The grand total cost for this PM forecast.
grandtotalcost.title: Grand Total Cost

glaccount.description: General ledger account to which costs on work orders generated from this PM are charged.
glaccount.title: GL Account

statusiface.description: Non persistent boolean field to indicate whether the status has been changed after the stateful object is fetched from the database.
statusiface.title: Has Status Changed

tuesday.description: Flag to indicate if this day is active for the PM
tuesday.title: Tuesday

pmactmeter.title: Generate Work Order Based on Meter Readings (Do Not Estimate)

frequnit.description: Frequency Units DAYS WEEKS MONTHS or YEARS
frequnit.title: Frequency Units

location.description: Location where work is done when work orders are generated from this PM.
location.title: Location

expectedlifedate.description: The date that the location reaches the expected useful life date. This date is calculated by using the location's original installation date and its expected useful life, which is defined by manufacturer or industry standard.
expectedlifedate.title: Expected EOL

reordfromstore.description: The storeroom that all the items in the current storeroom are reordered from.
reordfromstore.title: Reorder Storeroom

shiptolaborcode.description: Default labor code to ship items to when reorders are processed for this storeroom. Click the Select Value button to choose a labor code.
shiptolaborcode.title: Ship to Labor

storeroomparent.description: The parent storeroom of the current storeroom.
storeroomparent.title: Parent Storeroom

pluscduedate.description: Displays the date that the next calibration must be completed by.
pluscduedate.title: Next Calibration Due Date

bimusage.description: Describes how a location is used and controls COBie export behavior
bimusage.title: BIM Location type

isdefault.description: Specifies whether this storeroom is the default storeroom for the site. If the check box is selected, Maximo reserves items for this storeroom if a job plan or work order does not specify a storeroom. If the check box is cleared (the default), this storeroom is not used as the default storeroom when work orders are generated with no storeroom information.
isdefault.title: Default Storeroom

receiptvaracc.description: Debit account to track the variance between PO and Invoice exchange rates. This account contains the differences between the inventory standard cost and the receipt cost (specific to the storeroom) for any items in the associated storeroom location. It is used for standard costing only. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
receiptvaracc.title: Receipt Variance Account

reordfromstoresiteid.description: The site of the storeroom that all the items in the current storeroom are reordered from.
reordfromstoresiteid.title: Reorder Site

useinpopr.description: Specifies whether this storeroom can be used to supply and reserve inventory items and tools for use with internal purchase requisitions and internal purchase orders. If the check box is selected, you can order and reserve items or tools from this storeroom on internal POs and PRs. If the check box is cleared (the default), this storeroom cannot be used to supply items or tools internally.
useinpopr.title: Use in PO/PR

modelid.description: Import ID Typically from a BIM model or COBie data set
modelid.title: Import ID

pluscloop.description: Select this check box to identify the location as a loop calibration.
pluscloop.title: Loop Calibration

shiptoaddresscode.description: Default address code to ship items to when reorders are processed for this storeroom. Click the Select Value button to choose a shipping code.
shiptoaddresscode.title: Ship to Address

oldshrinkageacc.description: Last shrinkage cost account for manual adjustment for inventory quantity.
oldshrinkageacc.title: Old Shrinkage Account

estendoflife.description: The date when the location reaches its end of life. This date is based on the condition of the location.
estendoflife.title: Estimated EOL

purchvaracc.description: Account used to track fluctuations in purchase costs for items (or the variance between PO and original Last and Standard price). Not used with any MAXIMO transactions. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
purchvaracc.title: Purchase Variance Account

saddresscode.description: The address code identifies a service address. It must be unique by site for each service address.
saddresscode.title: Service Address

shrinkageacc.description: Credit account used when manually adjusting the inventory quantity. The transaction is positive when the actual inventory quantity is greater than the MAXIMO-calculated current balance. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
shrinkageacc.title: Shrinkage Account

oldinvcostadjacc.description: Last inventory cost adjustment account for manual inventory price adjustments.
oldinvcostadjacc.title: Old Invoice Cost Adjustment Account

description.description: Describes the storeroom location. To enter or view additional information, click the Long Description button.
description.title: Description

toolcontrolacc.description: Default debit account for capitalized tools. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
toolcontrolacc.title: Tool Control Account

pluscpmextdate.description: When this check box is selected, the Extended Date on the PM for the location was set. This check box is cleared when the work order is generated.
pluscpmextdate.title: Due Date Extended

invoicevaracc.description: Debit account used to track variances in the price, expressed in the vendor's currency, between receipt and invoice for this storeroom. The transaction amount is positive when the invoice line cost is greater than the receipt cost. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
invoicevaracc.title: Invoice Variance Account

bimroomname.description: The name or number of the room or space
bimroomname.title: Room Name/Number

expectedlife.description: Expected life of the location, expressed in years.  This value is based on the typical useful life that a manufacturer or industry standard recommends for a location or location type.
expectedlife.title: Expected Life

isrepairfacility.description: Specifies whether the location is a repair facility location. Repair facilities can take ownership of work orders from multiple sites in the same organization. User security can then be configured to give permissions to view work orders in multiple sites if the work orders are owned by the repair facility.
isrepairfacility.title: Is a Repair Facility

statusdate.description: Date the Locations status was last changed.
statusdate.title: Status Date

curvaracc.description: Account containing the differences between the PO price and the invoice price that result from changes in the exchange rate. If, as a result of an exchange rate change, the invoice cost in base currency exceeds the receipt cost in base currency, then the transaction amount is positive. If the invoice cost in base currency is less than the receipt cost in base currency, then the transaction amount is negative. This account is paired with the GL control account; when the currency variance account's value decreases, the inventory control account's value decreases. Variance accounts track price variances by storeroom location, not by item. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
curvaracc.title: Currency Variance Account

controlacc.description: Default GL account that Maximo applies to items in this storeroom during GL transactions such as issues, returns, receipts, transfers, and adjustments. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
controlacc.title: GL Control Account

invcostadjacc.description: Account that tracks changes in either the average price or the standard price from manual inventory price adjustments. These changes result from using the Adjust Average Cost and Adjust Standard Cost actions, respectively. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
invcostadjacc.title: Cost Adjustment Account

location.description: Storeroom where the item is stored.
location.title: Location

autowogen.description: Flag that indicates whether to start the wogen process when meter frequency is reached for a location
autowogen.title: Automatically Generate Work Orders

bimimportsrc.description: The external source for the data in this record such as a COBie sheet or an IFC class name
bimimportsrc.title: Import source

haschildren.description: Specifies whether the PM has any child PMs. If the check box is selected, the PM has child PMs associated with it. If the checkbox is cleared, the PM has no child PMs.
haschildren.title: Has Children

ACTION_SAVETHISQUERY.description: Request Schema for save this query

401.description:
BMXAA0033I Your session timed out due to inactivity.
BMXAA0026E The action could not be performed because the application server security is enabled. Proper authorization is required to perform this action. Report this error to your system administrator.
BMXAA7901E You cannot log in at this time. Contact the system administrator.
BMXAA0025E The method cannot be called because application server security is not enabled. Enable Application Server Security. For details, see information on defining server security in the information center.
BMXAA4122E You cannot be connected to an application server on the specified host. Ensure that an application server is running.
BMXAA0020E The session that is used to access business objects is either not valid or has timed out. Verify that the remote system is avaialble, then log in and authenticate again.
BMXAA0021E User name and password combination are not valid. Try again.
BMXAA0011E User {0} is inactive. Specify a different user.

412.description: BMXAA8229W Record {0} has been updated by another user. Your changes have not been saved. Refresh the record and try again.

413.description: BMXAA2249E The file being attached exceeds the size limit of {0}MB. See your system administrator to have the limit adjusted.

403.description:
BMXAA1671I Skip transaction.
BMXAA2283E Your password has expired.

404.description:
BMXAA4216E Unknown Object named {0} has been encountered. Gather the logs from the directory and determine the cause and source of the error.
BMXAA9510E The stable page resource was not found because the reload of a stable page was attempted or the idle expiry time has expired.
BMXAA4187E The relationship {0} does not exist for business object {1}. Verify that the relationship specified in parameter {0} exists in the Maxrelationship table.
BMXAA1283E The {0} value in the Consumed By field cannot be used. Define an object structure with a Consumed By value that is valid for the processing action.
BMXAA8727E The OSLC resource {0} with the ID {1} was not found as it does not exist in the system. In the database, verify whether the resource for the ID exists.

415.description: BMXAA8823E The format {0} is not supported. You must use a supported format such as application/json.

405.description: BMXAA8815E The HTTP method DELETE is not permitted on a collection resource. Only HTTP GET and POST methods are supported on collection URIs.

409.description: BMXAA9524E The transaction ID {1} already exists in the OSLC transaction table with resource ID {2} for object structure {3}.

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource.

tags: Maximo API for Preventive Maintenance (MXAPIPM) POST Actions

get.description: Get a (paged) collection of MXAPIPM based on the where filter (oslc.where) and select clause (oslc.select)
Get a MXAPIPM based on the id {id}

info.description: Maximo JSON API Wikis - Maximo JSON API Documents
info.title: Maximo RESTful API
